codeReview.summary: SecurityGate implementation is comprehensive and correct. It properly implements SAST scanning with Semgrep and CodeQL support, vulnerability parsing from both JSON formats, and blocking behavior for critical/high severity findings. All 20 unit tests pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly follows all Rust patterns documented in AGENTS.md: async_trait for async trait methods, proper error handling with Result<T, SwellError>, and structured logging with tracing. No deviations found.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SecurityGate impl uses #[async_trait] and spawn_blocking correctly for blocking command execution

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used 'Rust' skill (skillName from features.json). The implementation follows idiomatic Rust patterns including proper use of Option/Result types, iterator chains for parsing, and Arc<dyn Trait> for dynamic dispatch where needed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SecurityScannerType, FindingSeverity, Vulnerability, and SecurityScanResults all use proper Rust idioms

summary: SecurityGate feature PASSES review. Implementation correctly provides: (1) Security scanner support for Semgrep and CodeQL with fallback, (2) Vulnerability parsing from Semgrep JSON and CodeQL SARIF formats, (3) Blocking behavior for Error severity findings, (4) Graceful degradation when scanners unavailable. Build passes, 20 unit tests pass, clippy clean.
